element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Four distinct character classes available with different playstyles
Affects monster behavior and environmental consequences; good monsters only attack if player attacks first; evil monsters attack on sight
Ys-style combat system; player can choose to engage good or evil monsters strategically
Killing good monsters reduces MF; at zero MF, traps appear frequently across world
Character must eat twice daily and sleep regularly; neglecting needs gradually reduces HP and attack power
Every item including money has weight; exceeding LC causes movement slowdown
Wide variety of equipment and spells; banking system available; weight considerations central to progression
Killing any monster grants experience, money, and occasional equipment; good monster kills have MF penalty
Frequent trap appearance across world as consequence of poor morality choices
Found around Fairyland as reward for maintaining high moral standing

typical_relief_triggers: Maintaining high MF for positive item rewards
typical_tension_triggers: HP degradation from missed meals or sleep
Trap encounters triggered by low MF
Morality choice dilemmas (attacking good vs evil monsters)
Time pressure from day-night cycle mechanics
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady, survival-focused pace with resource management tension; puzzle and strategy-driven rather than action-intense; reviewers noted it requires sustained planning rather than reflexive action

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Good monsters may be avoided by player choice; rewards for high MF indicate optional areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate - player learns morality system, survival mechanics, and encumbrance management; requires strategic approach to monster encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Steady challenge - Resource management (food, sleep, weight) becomes increasingly demanding; morality choices accumulate consequences
difficulty_curve.late_game: Demanding - Reviewers cited game as 'very tough' requiring sustained strategy and planning; trap hazard frequency potentially severe with poor MF management
notable_spikes: Trap proliferation when MF reaches zero creates sudden difficulty spike
HP/attack power degradation from neglected survival needs accumulates into crisis points

notable_level_patterns.description: World populated with both good and evil monsters; player's engagement choices directly impact moral meter and environmental hazards; creates divergent difficulty paths based on playstyle
Time-based clock creates recurring biological needs (eating, sleeping) that force player to engage with towns, banks, and rest areas; missed deadlines degrade combat effectiveness
Encumbrance system forces strategic item selection; combined with survival needs and morality penalties, creates layered decision-making beyond simple combat optimization
